name: Patient Health Questionnaire-9
description: A self-administered depression screening and severity measure

questions.label: Little interest or pleasure in doing things?
Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much?
Feeling tired or having little energy?
Feeling bad about yourself — or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down?
Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television?
Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed? Or the opposite — being so fidgety or restless that you have been moving around a lot more than usual?
Thoughts that you would be better off dead, or of hurting yourself in some way?
If you checked off any problems, how difficult have these problems made it for you to do your work, take care of things at home, or get along with other people?

severityBands.label: Minimal Depression
severityBands.description: Symptoms suggest minimal or no depression

severityBands.label: Mild Depression
severityBands.description: Symptoms suggest mild depression; watchful waiting and repeat PHQ-9 at follow-up

severityBands.label: Moderate Depression
severityBands.description: Symptoms suggest moderate depression; treatment plan should be considered

severityBands.label: Moderately Severe Depression
severityBands.description: Symptoms suggest moderately severe depression; active treatment is recommended

severityBands.label: Severe Depression
severityBands.description: Symptoms suggest severe depression; immediate initiation of treatment is recommended

riskRules.message: PHQ-9 Q9 (suicidal ideation) score ≥ 2 indicates elevated suicide risk requiring clinical attention
PHQ-9 Q9 indicates presence of suicidal thoughts

interpretation.clinicalNotes: The PHQ-9 can be used to make a tentative depression diagnosis and monitor symptom severity over time. For a provisional diagnosis of Major Depressive Disorder: Q1 or Q2 must be answered 'More than half the days' or 'Nearly every day', AND five or more of Q1-Q9 total must be answered 'More than half the days' or 'Nearly every day' (count Q9 if present at all).

majorDepression.requires: Q1 or Q2 ≥ 2, AND 5+ questions from Q1-Q9 scored ≥ 2 (Q9 counts if ≥ 1)

otherDepression.requires: Q1 or Q2 ≥ 2, AND 2-4 questions from Q1-Q9 scored ≥ 2 (Q9 counts if ≥ 1)

functionalImpairment.description: Q10 assesses functional impairment and is not scored in the total but provides clinical context

levels.2: Moderate functional impairment - supports need for treatment
levels.3: Severe functional impairment - strongly supports need for treatment

actions.mild: Watchful waiting; repeat PHQ-9 at follow-up. Use clinical judgment about treatment based on duration of symptoms and functional impairment
actions.moderate: Treatment plan using antidepressant and/or psychotherapy. Consider counseling, follow-up and/or pharmacotherapy
actions.moderately-severe: Active treatment with pharmacotherapy and/or psychotherapy. Antidepressant and/or psychotherapy are indicated
actions.severe: Immediate initiation of pharmacotherapy and psychotherapy. If severe impairment, history of poor response, or if patient preference, refer to mental health specialist for treatment and/or collaborative management

monitoringGuidelines.description: PHQ-9 should be repeated at each visit to monitor treatment response
monitoringGuidelines.responseDefinition: A 50% reduction in PHQ-9 score from baseline indicates treatment response
monitoringGuidelines.treatmentGoal: Aim for remission (score < 5) within 8-12 weeks of treatment initiation

references: Kroenke K, Spitzer RL, Williams JB. The PHQ-9: validity of a brief depression severity measure. J Gen Intern Med. 2001;16(9):606-613.
Developed by Drs. Robert L. Spitzer, Janet B.W. Williams, Kurt Kroenke and colleagues
